title: OneRoster & Ed-Fi: the rails your SIS rides on
summary: A Student Information System is never an island — it exchanges rosters, grades, and reporting data with LMSs, assessment tools, and government agencies. Today: the two standards that make that exchange possible, OneRoster and Ed-Fi, plus the common vocabulary underneath, CEDS.

activities.title: Why an SIS needs interoperability standards
activities.markdown:
Your SIS is the **system of record** for students, enrollment, courses, and grades. But the data has to flow outward:

- to the **LMS** (Canvas, Moodle) so the right students show up in the right course;
- to **assessment** and analytics tools;
- to **state/agency reporting** systems.

Doing this with a custom integration per partner doesn't scale. Standards define a shared shape for the data so any compliant system can talk to any other. Three names dominate U.S. education:

- **OneRoster** (1EdTech) — exchanging roster info, course materials, and grades.
- **Ed-Fi** — a common data model + API connecting SIS, assessment, rostering, and LMS.
- **CEDS** — a national common *vocabulary* for education data elements.

activities.question: What does OneRoster primarily exchange between school systems?
options: Roster information, course materials, and grades (e.g., SIS ↔ LMS)
activities.explanation: OneRoster securely exchanges roster info, course materials, and grades between systems such as an SIS and an LMS. It supports both CSV bulk exchange and a real-time REST web-services mode.

activities.title: OneRoster 1.2 vs 1.1 — what changed

activities.question: OneRoster 1.2 reorganized the spec into three independent services. Which set?
activities.explanation: 1.2 split the standard into Rostering, Gradebook, and Resource services — each with its own REST and CSV patterns, so a vendor can implement just the parts it needs.

activities.title: How the data flows
activities.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  SIS[(SIS — system of record)]
  SIS -->|OneRoster Rostering| LMS[LMS / Canvas / Moodle]
  LMS -->|OneRoster Gradebook results| SIS
  SIS -->|Ed-Fi ODS/API| ODS[(Ed-Fi ODS)]
  ODS -->|reporting| STATE[State / Agency reporting]
  CEDS[[CEDS shared vocabulary]] -. defines elements .- SIS
  CEDS -. defines elements .- ODS

steps.title: SIS is the source of truth
steps.caption: Students, enrollment, courses, and grades live in the SIS.

steps.title: Push rosters via OneRoster
steps.caption: OneRoster Rostering provisions classes into the LMS; grades flow back.

steps.title: Feed the Ed-Fi ODS/API
steps.caption: The SIS also writes to the Ed-Fi Operational Data Store via its API.

steps.title: State / agency reporting
steps.caption: Reporting systems consume the Ed-Fi ODS.

steps.title: CEDS vocabulary underneath
steps.caption: Shared CEDS definitions keep every system's data comparable.

activities.caption: The SIS stays the source of truth; OneRoster moves rosters/grades to and from the LMS, while Ed-Fi's ODS/API feeds reporting. CEDS supplies the shared vocabulary.

activities.title: Ed-Fi: a common data language
activities.markdown:
**Ed-Fi** is an **open-source Data Standard + Unifying Data Model**, plus a technology suite (the **ODS/API** — Operational Data Store and REST API) that connects SIS, assessment, rostering, and LMS systems via one common data language.

- **Governance/funding:** a nonprofit initiative of the **Michael & Susan Dell Foundation**; the standard and API code are free under a license agreement.
- **Recent versions:** **Data Standard v6.0** shipped in late 2025 (supporting school years 2026‑27 / 2027‑28); the **Spring 2026** release added **Data Standard v6.1**, **ODS/API v7.3.2**, and a new **Ed-Fi OneRoster Service**.

activities.question: Ed-Fi is a nonprofit initiative backed by which foundation?
activities.explanation: Ed-Fi is a nonprofit initiative of the Michael & Susan Dell Foundation; its data standard and API code are made available for free under a license agreement.

activities.title: Ed-Fi adoption (historical, U.S. states/agencies)
activities.caption: Reported counts of U.S. states / state agencies using Ed-Fi. These are 2019 and 2020 figures — treat as historical, not current; no single official current state-count was found.

sources.title: Why the Ed-Fi data standard is taking over — EdScoop

activities.front: What is **CEDS**, and who maintains it?
activities.back: **Common Education Data Standards** — a national common vocabulary / data dictionary of shared education data elements spanning Early Learning, K‑12, Postsecondary, and P‑20W. Maintained by **NCES** (National Center for Education Statistics), part of the U.S. Department of Education.

activities.headline: Ed-Fi and 1EdTech ship the Ed-Fi OneRoster Service
activities.summary: In its **Spring 2026** release (May 11, 2026), the Ed-Fi Alliance introduced the **Ed-Fi OneRoster Service**, which implements 1EdTech's **OneRoster v1.2 API natively on the Ed-Fi ODS/API**. Agencies can now serve a OneRoster-compliant endpoint directly from data they already hold in Ed-Fi — a concrete alignment between the two leading U.S. education data standards.

items: **OneRoster** (1EdTech) exchanges rosters, materials, and grades; **v1.2** splits into Rostering, Gradebook, and Resource services.
**Ed-Fi** is an open-source data standard + ODS/API, backed by the Michael & Susan Dell Foundation; latest is Data Standard v6.1 / ODS-API v7.3.2 (Spring 2026).
**CEDS** (NCES) provides the shared vocabulary both lean on.
The 2026 **Ed-Fi OneRoster Service** lets one system speak both standards — a pattern an enterprise SIS should plan for.

glossary.definition: A 1EdTech standard for securely exchanging roster info, course materials, and grades between systems (e.g., SIS ↔ LMS), via CSV bulk or REST. v1.2 splits into Rostering, Gradebook, and Resource services.

An open-source education Data Standard + Unifying Data Model plus the ODS/API technology suite, backed by the Michael & Susan Dell Foundation, used to connect SIS, assessment, rostering, and LMS data.
Common Education Data Standards — a national common vocabulary/data dictionary of shared education data elements, maintained by NCES (U.S. Dept of Education).
Ed-Fi's Operational Data Store and its REST API — the technology suite that stores and moves data shaped by the Ed-Fi Data Standard.
Provisioning students, teachers, and classes into systems. OneRoster's Rostering service standardizes this exchange.

reflection: Where in ManageArk would a OneRoster Gradebook endpoint or an Ed-Fi ODS/API surface make the platform more interoperable than Banner or Workday? Which would you build first, and why?
